title: A Family Dinner
summary: A woman describes a special family dinner she organised at her home. She talks about the food she prepared, the guests who came, and the happy atmosphere of the evening.
body: Last Sunday I organised a family dinner at my house. It was a special occasion because my brother had just come back from working abroad for six months. I wanted to make it a memorable evening, so I spent most of Saturday preparing the food. I made a large pot of chicken soup to start, followed by roast chicken with vegetables and roast potatoes. For dessert I baked a chocolate cake because it is my brother's favourite. My sister came over in the afternoon to help me set the table and decorate the dining room with some flowers and candles. By seven o'clock everyone had arrived. There were eight of us altogether — my parents, my two sisters, my brother, his girlfriend, and me. We sat around the table for nearly three hours, eating, laughing, and catching up on news. My father gave a short speech and welcomed my brother home. Everyone agreed that the chicken soup was delicious. My brother said it was the best home-cooked meal he had eaten in months. We finished the evening with tea and my chocolate cake. I felt so happy to have everyone together in one place. I hope we can do it again soon.

annotations.exampleEn: We go to a nice restaurant for special occasions like birthdays and anniversaries.
She spent two years working abroad before returning home.
The trip to the mountains was truly memorable.
They had roast beef and roast potatoes for Sunday lunch.
Could you set the table while I finish cooking?

annotations.meaningVi: hỏi thăm tin tức (của nhau sau một thời gian xa nhau)
annotations.exampleEn: We spent the evening catching up on news with old friends.

annotations.meaningVi: nói một bài phát biểu ngắn
annotations.exampleEn: The manager gave a short speech to welcome the new employee.
After weeks of travelling, he was looking forward to a home-cooked meal.
There were twelve of us altogether at the party.
She lit some candles to create a warm atmosphere at the dinner table.

annotations.phrase: I hope we can do it again soon
annotations.meaningVi: Tôi hy vọng chúng ta có thể làm lại điều đó sớm
annotations.pronunciation: /aɪ həʊp wiː kæn duː ɪt əˈɡen suːn/
annotations.exampleEn: We had such a great time — I hope we can do it again soon.

grammarNotes.title: Past perfect for earlier past action: 'had just come back', 'had arrived'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ hoàn thành (had + past participle) dùng để chỉ một hành động xảy ra TRƯỚC một hành động khác trong quá khứ. 'My brother had just come back from working abroad.' — Anh trai tôi vừa mới trở về từ nước ngoài (trước khi tôi tổ chức bữa tối). 'By seven o'clock everyone had arrived.' — Vào lúc bảy giờ mọi người đã đến (trước bảy giờ). 'had just + past participle' diễn tả hành động xảy ra ngay trước thời điểm nói về.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The past perfect (had + past participle) is used to describe an action that happened before another past action. 'My brother had just come back from working abroad' — he returned before the dinner was organised. 'By seven o'clock everyone had arrived' — arrival happened before or at seven. The word 'by' with a past time often signals the past perfect. Also: 'the best meal he had eaten in months' — he had been abroad for months and eating various meals; this was the best of all of them.

grammarNotes.title: Sequencing past events: 'to start', 'followed by', 'for dessert'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả các bữa ăn hoặc chuỗi sự kiện, chúng ta dùng các cụm từ để sắp xếp thứ tự. 'I made a large pot of chicken soup to start' — để bắt đầu. 'followed by roast chicken' — tiếp theo là. 'For dessert I baked a chocolate cake.' — Tráng miệng là. Các cụm này giúp người nghe hình dung trình tự của bữa ăn hoặc sự kiện mà không cần dùng các số thứ tự như 'first, second, third'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: When describing a meal or sequence of events, use linking phrases to show order: 'to start' (the first course), 'followed by' (the next course or event), 'for dessert' (the final sweet course). These are more natural than 'first, second, third' in food descriptions. In the narrative, time expressions also organise events: 'Last Sunday' (when it happened), 'most of Saturday' (preparation), 'in the afternoon' (when her sister came), 'By seven o'clock' (when guests arrived).

translationVi: Chủ nhật tuần trước tôi tổ chức một bữa tối gia đình tại nhà. Đây là một dịp đặc biệt vì anh trai tôi vừa mới trở về sau sáu tháng làm việc ở nước ngoài. Tôi muốn tạo ra một buổi tối đáng nhớ nên tôi dành phần lớn thứ bảy để chuẩn bị thức ăn. Tôi nấu một nồi súp gà lớn để bắt đầu, tiếp theo là gà quay với rau củ và khoai tây nướng. Tráng miệng là tôi nướng bánh chocolate vì đó là món yêu thích của anh trai. Em gái tôi đến vào buổi chiều để giúp tôi dọn bàn và trang trí phòng ăn với hoa và nến. Vào lúc bảy giờ tất cả mọi người đã đến. Tổng cộng có tám người — bố mẹ tôi, hai em gái, anh trai, bạn gái của anh ấy và tôi. Chúng tôi ngồi quanh bàn gần ba tiếng đồng hồ, ăn uống, cười đùa và hỏi thăm tin tức của nhau. Bố tôi nói một bài phát biểu ngắn và chào đón anh trai trở về nhà. Mọi người đều đồng ý rằng súp gà rất ngon. Anh trai nói đó là bữa ăn nấu tại nhà ngon nhất anh ấy đã ăn trong nhiều tháng. Chúng tôi kết thúc buổi tối bằng trà và bánh chocolate của tôi. Tôi rất hạnh phúc khi có tất cả mọi người ở cùng một nơi. Tôi hy vọng chúng tôi có thể làm lại điều đó sớm.

questions.prompt: When did the family dinner take place?
questions.explanation: 'Last Sunday I organised a family dinner at my house.'
questions.hint: The first sentence gives the day.

questions.prompt: Why was the dinner a special occasion?
options: The narrator got a new job
Her brother had just come back from working abroad
questions.explanation: 'my brother had just come back from working abroad for six months'
questions.hint: The reason is connected to a family member's return.

questions.prompt: When did she prepare the food?
questions.explanation: 'I spent most of Saturday preparing the food.'
'I made a large pot of chicken soup to start.'
questions.hint: 'To start' means it was the first course.

options: Roast chicken with vegetables and roast potatoes
questions.explanation: 'followed by roast chicken with vegetables and roast potatoes'

questions.prompt: Why did she bake a chocolate cake for dessert?
options: Because it is the easiest cake to make
Because everyone in the family loves it
Because it is her brother's favourite
Because she had all the ingredients
questions.explanation: 'I baked a chocolate cake because it is my brother's favourite.'
questions.hint: She chose it to please a specific person.

questions.prompt: What did her sister help with?
options: She helped cook the main course
She set the table and decorated the dining room
She bought the flowers and candles
She welcomed the guests at the door
questions.explanation: 'My sister came over in the afternoon to help me set the table and decorate the dining room.'

questions.prompt: How many people were at the dinner?
Who was NOT mentioned as a guest?
questions.explanation: The guests were: parents, two sisters, brother, his girlfriend, and the narrator. No cousin is mentioned.
questions.hint: Count the named people and spot the one not in the list.

questions.prompt: How long did they sit around the table?
questions.explanation: 'We sat around the table for nearly three hours.'

questions.prompt: What did her father do during the dinner?
options: He gave a short speech to welcome her brother
questions.explanation: 'My father gave a short speech and welcomed my brother home.'
questions.hint: He made a brief public statement.

questions.prompt: What did her brother say about the food?
options: He said the cake was too sweet
He said the chicken was overcooked
He said it was the best home-cooked meal he had eaten in months
He said the soup was a little salty
questions.explanation: 'My brother said it was the best home-cooked meal he had eaten in months.'
questions.hint: He used a superlative to describe the meal.

cloze.template: Last Sunday I organised a family {{b1}} at my house. It was a special occasion because my brother had just come back from working {{b2}} for six months. I wanted to make it a {{b3}} evening, so I spent most of Saturday preparing the food. I made a large pot of chicken {{b4}} to start, followed by roast chicken with vegetables and roast potatoes. For {{b5}} I baked a chocolate cake because it is my brother's favourite. My sister came over in the afternoon to help me {{b6}} the table and decorate the dining room with some flowers and {{b7}}. By seven o'clock everyone had {{b8}}. There were eight of us {{b9}}. We sat around the table for nearly three hours, eating, laughing, and {{b10}} on news. My father gave a short {{b11}} and welcomed my brother home. My brother said it was the best home-cooked {{b12}} he had eaten in months.

blanks.explanation: 'dinner' — the passage describes a family dinner.
'abroad' — working abroad means working in another country.
'memorable' — she wanted it to be an evening they would remember.
'soup' — chicken soup was the first course.
'dessert' — dessert is the sweet course served at the end of a meal.
'set' — 'set the table' means to arrange the plates, cutlery, and glasses.
'candles' — she decorated with flowers and candles.
'arrived' — by seven o'clock, all guests had arrived.
'altogether' — 'eight of us altogether' means eight people in total.
'catching' — 'catching up on news' means sharing updates about life.
'speech' — 'gave a short speech' means he spoke briefly to the group.
'meal' — a home-cooked meal is food prepared and eaten at home.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator organised a special dinner to celebrate her brother's return home. Family gatherings like this are very important in many cultures. Why do you think shared meals are an important tradition in many families and cultures around the world? Do you have a similar tradition in your family?
